rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical Y(OTf)3-catalyzed selenofunctionalization of styrenes and thiophenes with diphenyl diselenide to give 1,1-diaryl-2-phenylselenyl products (4a) and 2-phenylselenyl thiophenes (5a)
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of PhSeSePh (or PhSe-) to generate an electrophilic PhSe+ species (or PhSe radical), which is activated/Lewis-acid-promoted by Y(CF3CO2)3 to engage the styrene/thiophene

key_intermediates.name: benzylic carbocation after thiophene attack on seleniranium

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes PhSeSePh (assisted by Y(CF3CO2)3 as Lewis acid) to an electrophilic PhSe+ equivalent, which adds to styrene forming a seleniranium ion; nucleophilic attack by 2-ethylthiophene at the benzylic position opens the seleniranium to give 4a, while direct electrophilic selenylation of 2-methylthiophene affords 5a. Protons are reduced at the Pt cathode to H2.
notes: Only the gram-scale procedure and a time-course (kinetic) study were shown on this page; mechanistic probes (radical traps, CV, intermediate isolation) are not visible here, so the mechanism is inferred from the reaction setup (electrochemistry, Y(III) Lewis acid, diselenide, styrene/thiophene). A radical pathway via PhSe• cannot be excluded from this page alone.
